scenario: Sable Bank ingests interest rates with 18 digits of precision into a Camunda 8.8 form for a back-office task averaging 600 transactions per day, and the modeler is evaluating the Serialize to string property on the Number element to preserve that precision when writing the bound variable rate at form submission time.
question: What effect does enabling Serialize to string on a Camunda 8.8 Number element have on the value written to the bound variable?

options.text: Enabling Serialize to string lets the Number element emit the value with unlimited precision digits, configuring the output format used when the runtime writes the bound variable on submission
Enabling Serialize to string forces the value into a 16-digit double-precision float, capping the bound variable at the floating-point precision allowed by the form runtime on submission
Enabling Serialize to string rounds the value to the configured Decimal digits setting before writing the bound variable, dropping any extra precision typed by the user at submit time
Enabling Serialize to string converts the value into a localised currency string before writing the bound variable, using the form locale to format the precision shown to the user

a.text: Correct. The Configurable properties section states that Serialize to string configures the output format of the value and enables unlimited precision digits, which is exactly the behaviour the modeler needs to preserve an 18-digit input on the bound variable.

b.text: Incorrect. The documentation describes Serialize to string as unlocking unlimited precision digits, not as constraining the value to a 16-digit double-precision float on output.

c.text: Incorrect. Serialize to string does not couple its behaviour to the Decimal digits setting; its documented effect is to enable unlimited precision digits in the output format of the value.

d.text: Incorrect. The Number element does not perform locale-aware currency formatting; Serialize to string changes the output format to unlock unlimited precision, not to localise the value at the submit step.

explanation: The Serialize to string property on a Number element changes how the runtime emits the bound value, enabling unlimited precision digits in the output. It is the documented mechanism for preserving precision beyond floating-point limits when persisting the variable on submission.
